mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization approaching limits, triggering inflation fears and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO rises during tightening periods, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent directional flip.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with SPX 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, markets price in imminent central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and stagnant unemployment indicates inefficient labor absorption, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive SPX returns in more than 65% of cases, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, expanding industrial production signals robust capacity growth without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when INDPRO rises alongside low rates, or the regime condition fails to isolate a distinct predictive regime.
